audit_finding.interpreter_state: self/runtime.c:21-25 ALREADY has setvbuf(stdout/stderr, _IOLBF). Line-buffered, flushes on \n.
audit_finding.println_impl: self/runtime.c:3100 — `void hexa_println(HexaVal v) { hexa_print_val(v); printf("\n"); }`. No explicit fflush — relies on _IOLBF to flush on \n.
audit_finding.verdict: interpreter side appears CORRECT. Stdout suppression is transport-layer.

transport_layer_hypothesis.h1_ssh_no_pty: hexa_remote uses ssh without -t; remote stdout is pipe-buffered, flushes on connection close — partial-line buffers may be lost
transport_layer_hypothesis.h2_docker_exec_no_tty: resolver uses `docker exec -i` (no -t); stdout pipe not flushed before container exec exits
transport_layer_hypothesis.h3_remote_collector_swallows: hexa_remote's command capture may redirect stdout to a log file rather than passing through

revised_patches.approach: interpreter: add fflush(stdout) after each hexa_println (paranoid)
revised_patches.diff: void hexa_println(HexaVal v) { hexa_print_val(v); printf("\n"); fflush(stdout); }
revised_patches.risk: low — slight write overhead per println
revised_patches.reason_not_applied: rebuild affects every script in workspace; deferred to user review

revised_patches.risk: medium — pty allocation can break batch pipelines / interactive prompts

revised_patches.approach: transport: docker exec -t flag in resolver

revised_patches.risk: low — stdbuf is GNU coreutils, may not be in container image

applied_status: NONE applied this session — all blocked on user review for transport-layer behavior change
interim_workaround: println-to-file convention via exec() heredoc (already de facto for ω-audio synthesis)
session_decision: R2.2 patch axis remains LOW priority since (a) interpreter is already correct, (b) transport-layer fix has medium UX risk (pty issues in batch), (c) the file-based evidence pattern works
